(August 20, 1954 – June 6, 2016) was an American actress, activist, and writer. She is known for her role as Rachel Scali, the wife of Police Commissioner Tony Scali, in the 1990s television series *[The Commish](/wiki/The_Commish "The Commish")*, for which she received a [Golden Globe](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award "Golden Globe Award") nomination for Best Performance by an Actress in a Supporting Role, in 1994. Major film roles include Lenora LaMotta, the wife of [Joe Pesci](/wiki/Joe_Pesci "Joe Pesci")'s character in the 1980 film *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")*, and as a [Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles") fan in [Robert Zemeckis](/wiki/Robert_Zemeckis "Robert Zemeckis")' 1978 [Beatlemania](/wiki/Beatlemania "Beatlemania") ensemble *[I Wanna Hold Your Hand](/wiki/I_Wanna_Hold_Your_Hand_(film) "I Wanna Hold Your Hand (film)")*. She was also known for raising public awareness of the crime of [stalking](/wiki/Stalking "Stalking") after surviving a knife attack by an obsessed fan at her home in 1982.

American actress (1954–2016)

| Theresa Saldana | |

**Theresa Saldana** (August 20, 1954 – June 6, 2016) was an American actress, activist, and writer. She is known for her role as Rachel Scali, the wife of Police Commissioner Tony Scali, in the 1990s television series *[The Commish](/wiki/The_Commish "The Commish")*, for which she received a [Golden Globe](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award "Golden Globe Award") nomination for Best Performance by an Actress in a Supporting Role, in 1994. Major film roles include Lenora LaMotta, the wife of [Joe Pesci](/wiki/Joe_Pesci "Joe Pesci")'s character in the 1980 film *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")*, and as a [Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles") fan in [Robert Zemeckis](/wiki/Robert_Zemeckis "Robert Zemeckis")' 1978 [Beatlemania](/wiki/Beatlemania "Beatlemania") ensemble *[I Wanna Hold Your Hand](/wiki/I_Wanna_Hold_Your_Hand_(film) "I Wanna Hold Your Hand (film)")*. She was also known for raising public awareness of the crime of [stalking](/wiki/Stalking "Stalking") after surviving a knife attack by an obsessed fan at her home in 1982.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Theresa_Saldana&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Saldana was born in the [Brooklyn borough](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn") of New York City, and was adopted at five days old by Divina and Tony Saldana, a family of Puerto Rican and Italian-American heritage.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Saldana took dance lessons as a child. After suffering a serious shoulder injury while part of a tumbling team, she enrolled in acting classes at age 12. After being spotted by a talent scout while performing in an [Off Broadway](/wiki/Off_Broadway "Off Broadway") musical called *The New York City Street Show* in 1977, she was cast in the 1978 film *[Nunzio](/wiki/Nunzio_(film) "Nunzio (film)")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Stalking incident

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Theresa_Saldana&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Stalking incident")]

On March 15, 1982, Saldana was stalked by Arthur Richard Jackson, a 46-year-old [drifter](/wiki/Vagrancy "Vagrancy") from [Aberdeen](/wiki/Aberdeen "Aberdeen"), Scotland. Jackson became obsessed with Saldana after seeing her in the 1980 films *[Defiance](/wiki/Defiance_(1980_film) "Defiance (1980 film)")* and *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")*.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-larryking-5) He hired a [private investigator](/wiki/Private_investigator "Private investigator") who obtained the unlisted phone number of Saldana's mother. Jackson then called Saldana's mother and posed as [Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese "Martin Scorsese")'s assistant, saying he needed Saldana's residential address in order to contact her for replacing an actress in a film role in Europe.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/Making_of_ILLUSION_INFINITY%2C_director_Roger_Steinmann_with_Theresa_Saldana.jpg/250px-Making_of_ILLUSION_INFINITY%2C_director_Roger_Steinmann_with_Theresa_Saldana.jpg)](/wiki/File:Making_of_ILLUSION_INFINITY,_director_Roger_Steinmann_with_Theresa_Saldana.jpg)

Saldana on the set of *Illusion Infinity*, with its filmmaker Roger Steinmann

Jackson approached Saldana in front of her [West Hollywood](/wiki/West_Hollywood,_California "West Hollywood, California") residence in broad daylight on March 15, 1982 and repeatedly stabbed her with a 5+1⁄2-inch (14 cm) hunting knife, puncturing a lung. His attack was so fierce that the blade bent. Although there were many nearby onlookers, including children,[[5]](#cite_note-larryking-5) the attack was interrupted only when a deliveryman, Jeff Fenn, intervened after hearing Saldana's cries, rushed from the second floor of an apartment building, and incapacitated Jackson. Following the assault, Saldana was hospitalized with 10 stab wounds and underwent a four-month hospital stay at the [Motion Picture Hospital](/wiki/Motion_Picture_%26_Television_Fund "Motion Picture & Television Fund"). She relived the incident in the television film *[Victims for Victims: The Theresa Saldana Story](/wiki/Victims_for_Victims:_The_Theresa_Saldana_Story "Victims for Victims: The Theresa Saldana Story")* and again in an episode of *[Hunter](/wiki/Hunter_(1984_American_TV_series) "Hunter (1984 American TV series)")*.

Jackson served almost 14 years in prison for the assault and making subsequent threats against Saldana and her rescuer while in prison. He was then extradited to the United Kingdom in 1996 to be tried for a 1966 robbery and murder. Jackson (who once saw himself as "the benevolent [angel of death](/wiki/Angel_of_mercy_(criminology) "Angel of mercy (criminology)")") was found not guilty by diminished responsibility in 1997 and committed to a British psychiatric hospital, where he died of heart failure in 2004, at the age of 68.

Jackson's method of finding and approaching Saldana inspired stalker [Robert John Bardo](/wiki/Robert_John_Bardo "Robert John Bardo") to hire a private investigator to contact [Rebecca Schaeffer](/wiki/Rebecca_Schaeffer "Rebecca Schaeffer"), a young TV sitcom star whom he subsequently murdered, also in West Hollywood, on July 18, 1989.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Victim advocacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Theresa_Saldana&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Victim advocacy")]

Following her long recovery, Saldana founded the Victims for Victims organization and participated in lobbying for the 1990 anti-stalking law and the 1994 [Driver's Privacy Protection Act](/wiki/Driver%27s_Privacy_Protection_Act "Driver's Privacy Protection Act"), both of which came into being partly as a consequence of the attack. The experience also inspired Saldana to play herself in the television film *[Victims for Victims: The Theresa Saldana Story](/wiki/Victims_for_Victims:_The_Theresa_Saldana_Story "Victims for Victims: The Theresa Saldana Story")*, and she authored the book *Beyond Survival*, a memoir of her experiences after being attacked.

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Theresa_Saldana&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Saldana's first marriage was to Fred Feliciano from February 11, 1979 to 1986. In March 1989, she married for the second time, to Phil Peters, with whom she had a daughter, Tianna Saldana Peters (born August 30, 1989, in Los Angeles). They remained together until her death.

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Theresa_Saldana&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Death")]

Saldana died on June 6, 2016, at age 61, after being hospitalized with [pneumonia](/wiki/Pneumonia "Pneumonia")[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) at [Cedars-Sinai Medical Center](/wiki/Cedars-Sinai_Medical_Center "Cedars-Sinai Medical Center"). [Michael Chiklis](/wiki/Michael_Chiklis "Michael Chiklis"), who played Saldana's husband on *[The Commish](/wiki/The_Commish "The Commish")*, wrote that it was "painful to hear the news."[[9]](#cite_note-9)
